# UC-13.7.5 · Fluentd Memory Buffer Utilization Approaching Limit

## Description

Memory buffers protect against short downstream outages; running near the ceiling leaves no headroom for bursts and precedes blocking `emit` calls. Percent-of-limit tracking triggers proactive scaling.

## Value

Avoids tail drops on bursty services like ingress controllers during attacks or viral traffic.

## Implementation

Some plugins expose only chunk counts—map to bytes via avg chunk size calibration. Combine with `sourcetype=fluentd:log` overflow messages for corroboration.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| eval used=coalesce(buffer_queue_length, buffer_stage_length, queue_length)
| eval lim=coalesce(buffer_total_limit, buffer_chunk_limit, total_limit)
| eval pct=round(100*used/nullif(lim,0),1)
| where pct > 75 OR used > 0 AND lim-used < 500
| stats latest(pct) as buf_pct, latest(used) as q, latest(lim) as cap by host, plugin_id
| sort -buf_pct
```

## Visualization

Gauge charts per plugin, heatmap (host × buffer pct), alert table.

## References

- [Fluentd — Buffer section parameters](https://docs.fluentd.org/configuration/buffer-section)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
